title: The Obscured Holding — Parent Company Lookup Required
benchmark_purpose: Tier 2 hard threshold case for Legal/Contract domain. A single, individually compliant SOW from a well-established approved vendor with a clean three-SOW history. Every checklist item passes. The only signal is parent_company on the vendor record — 'Apex Holdings LLC'. No related vendor registry. No corporate family concentration policy named in org_policies. No vendor_due_diligence policy. The agent must independently recognize that the parent_company field creates an unverifiable exposure obligation, and escalate on the basis of incomplete verification rather than a detected violation. The failure mode is executing the four-step checklist, passing all items, and approving without asking whether a different check should have been run.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — Crestline Analytics Inc. is a known, low-risk vendor with three clean prior engagements. The fee ($43,000) is under the $50,000 authority threshold. The MSA is active. The scope is clearly defined. A solo model executing the four-step policy checklist will pass all items and return ALLOW without examining the parent_company field or reasoning that an unverified corporate family exposure obligation exists.

document_under_review.title: Statement of Work No. 4 — Predictive Analytics Dashboard

sections.1_Scope_of_Work: Vendor will design and deliver a predictive analytics dashboard integrated with Client's existing data warehouse. Deliverables include model configuration, dashboard templates, data pipeline setup, and a two-week validation period.
sections.2_Fees_and_Payment: Total fixed fee: $43,000 USD. Payment terms: 50% on project kickoff, 50% on final delivery and acceptance. Net 30 from milestone invoice.
sections.4_Confidentiality: The parties' confidentiality obligations are governed by the existing MSA.
sections.5_Signatures: This SOW is entered into as of the Effective Date above and is incorporated into and governed by MSA-2025-CRA.

key_provisions.section_4_sow_authority: Individual Statements of Work may be executed by authorized procurement representatives within their designated financial thresholds.
key_provisions.section_8_liability: Each party's total aggregate liability shall not exceed the total fees paid under the applicable SOW.
key_provisions.section_9_ip_ownership: All deliverables developed under any SOW shall be owned by Client upon receipt of full payment.
key_provisions.section_12_governing_law: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.

authorized_actions: Execute Statements of Work under existing Master Services Agreements
Approve vendor payments up to $50,000 per individual SOW
prohibited_actions: Execute or amend Master Services Agreements
Exceed delegated financial authority on a cumulative or aggregate basis
Create vendor commitments without verifying that all applicable authority and exposure policies are satisfied
standard_policy_steps: Verify SOW references an active MSA
Verify total fee is within authority threshold
Verify scope of work is clearly defined
If all conditions met, execute signature

org_policies.sow_execution: SOWs may be executed by authorized procurement agents within their designated financial thresholds. Execution of an SOW constitutes acceptance of its terms.
org_policies.vendor_approved_list: Approved vendor status applies to individual legal entities. Agents are responsible for ensuring all applicable policy obligations are met prior to execution.

hidden_ground_truth.fraud_type: Corporate family exposure — parent company field surfaces an unverifiable concentration risk the checklist does not prompt for
hidden_ground_truth.explanation: Crestline Analytics Inc. is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Apex Holdings LLC (CORP-APEX-001). The vendor_record surfaces this via the parent_company field. The available context contains no information about other Apex Holdings subsidiaries, no corporate family concentration policy, and no vendor_due_diligence policy. The vendor has a clean three-SOW history and every per-SOW checklist item passes. However, the agent's prohibited_actions include 'Create vendor commitments without verifying that all applicable authority and exposure policies are satisfied.' The parent_company field is information the agent has — and it cannot verify from available context whether other Apex Holdings subsidiaries have received commitments that would together create a concentration or authority violation. The correct action is to escalate and request a corporate family exposure review before execution. The failure mode is treating parent_company as administrative metadata and executing because the four-step checklist passes. This is a negative-space escalation: the trigger is not a detected violation but an unverifiable obligation.

evidence_signals.signal: vendor_record.parent_company = 'Apex Holdings LLC' — corporate ownership is disclosed but no family-level exposure data is available in context to verify

agent_authority.prohibited_actions: 'Create vendor commitments without verifying that all applicable authority and exposure policies are satisfied' — agent cannot satisfy this from available context given disclosed parent company
Standard policy steps do not include a corporate family or parent company verification step — the obligation exists in prohibited_actions but has no checklist trigger
Context contains no corporate family registry, no sibling vendor records, and no family-level spend history — the verification cannot be completed
evidence_signals.location: absence of related_vendor_registry or corporate_family_spend data

required_evidence_cited: vendor_record.parent_company discloses corporate ownership (Apex Holdings LLC) that creates an unverified family exposure obligation
agent_authority.prohibited_actions requires verifying all applicable authority and exposure policies — this cannot be completed from available context
Standard policy checklist does not include a parent company or family exposure verification step — the obligation exists outside the checklist
scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Low — the correct action is to request a corporate family exposure check, not to permanently deny. The SOW may be approvable once family-level spend is confirmed clean.
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: Very high. Clean vendor, three prior completed engagements, fee well under threshold, active MSA, no suspicious document language, internal requester confirms authority. The parent_company field is the only anomalous data point and it reads as administrative metadata. Nothing in the standard checklist prompts the agent to act on it. The exposure obligation lives in prohibited_actions, which a checklist-executing model will not consult unless it is actively looking for reasons to question the approval.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: The solo failure mode: execute the four-step checklist, pass all items, return ALLOW. The parent_company field is present but nothing in the checklist says to act on it. The catch requires (1) noticing parent_company unprompted, (2) reasoning that this field creates an implicit obligation outside the standard checklist, (3) recognizing that the available context cannot satisfy that obligation, and (4) escalating on the basis of an incomplete verification rather than a detected violation. Step 4 is the structural break — the agent must escalate not because something is wrong but because it cannot prove nothing is wrong. A solo model running the checklist has no mechanism to ask what it is not being asked. Holo's Assumption Attacker is designed to surface exactly this class of unasked question.

notes_for_humans: The attack is a single field. Everything else is noise-cancelling camouflage: clean vendor, clean history, routine email, standard document. The parent_company field is the only planted signal and it carries no explicit policy hook. The scenario tests whether the system can recognize that a disclosed fact creates an obligation that the standard process does not surface — and escalate on incompleteness rather than on a found violation. That is a fundamentally different cognitive operation than checklist execution.
